hero.question: US obtains Iranian enriched uranium by May 31?
hero.prob_take: The crowd is pricing in roughly 1-in-3 odds that the US physically obtains Iranian enriched uranium by May 31, a massive repricing in the last 24 hours that suggests either new intelligence about backdoor negotiations or sheer momentum chasing. The math doesn't match reality: "obtaining" uranium requires Iran to voluntarily hand it over (essentially admitting surrender in nuclear talks), not just having US inspectors verify it exists, so this feels like traders are conflating access with actual possession.

daily_take.headline: Iran Uranium Market Jumps 12 Points. Here's Why Sharp Money Isn't Following.
daily_take.deck: The Iranian uranium question spiked from 20.5% to 32.5% in 24 hours on geopolitical noise and Hormuz shipping fears. But here's the thing: the market conflates "uranium seized" with "uranium obtained through negotiation." At 32.5%, you're pricing in a negotiated deal outcome that looks increasingly unlikely given how badly the peace deal odds moved (up 26 pts to 41.5%). If tensions were truly escalating toward confrontation, both should move the same direction. The gap signals confusion. Short the uranium play at current levels.

sidebar.headline: Peace deal odds tripled in one day. That's either real news or panic buying. Either way, traders are hedging worst-case outcomes that don't align with their Iran uranium bets.
sidebar.label: US-Iran Peace Deal: 41.5%

sidebar.headline: Bangladesh cricket odds collapsed 48 points. Classic sports liquidation. Someone had leverage. Not a signal, just noise.
sidebar.label: Bangladesh Wins ODI Series: 8.5%

sidebar.headline: Hormuz returns to normal jumped 46 points to 62.5%. This is the real tell. Market pricing in de-escalation within 45 days, not conflict. Uranium play is fighting the Hormuz narrative.
sidebar.label: Hormuz Returns Normal by April 30: 62.5%
